creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.
contentSnippet: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.

creator: Marina Temkin, Jagmeet Singh
title: (No AI) Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
dc:creator: Marina Temkin, Jagmeet Singh
content: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.
contentSnippet: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.

title: (No AI) Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
author: Elizabeth Lopatto
content:
![Elon Musk in front of a background of court gavels.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STK022_ELON_MUSK_CVIRGINIA4_H.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Just a simple country CEO who was being tricked by that sophisticated lawyer | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images

About five hours into Elon Musk's testimony, I typed the following sentence into my notes: "I have never been more sympathetic to Sam Altman in my life."

Musk's direct testimony was an improvement over yesterday - even if his lawyer kept asking leading questions to cue him in how to answer. But that memory was immediately obliterated by an absolutely miserable cross-examination. For hours, Musk refused to answer yes or no questions with yes or no, occasionally "forgot" things he'd testified to in the morning, and scolded defense lawyer William Savitt. I watched a few jury members glance at each other. During one testy exchange, one woman wa …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/921022/elon-musk-cross-openai-altman)

Musk's direct testimony was an improvement over yesterday - even if his lawyer kept asking leading questions to cue him in how to answer. But that memory was immediately obliterated by an absolutely miserable cross-examination. For hours, Musk refused to answer yes or no questions with yes or no, occasionally "forgot" things he'd testified to in the morning, and scolded defense lawyer William Savitt. I watched a few jury members glance at each other. During one testy exchange, one woman wa …

title: (No AI) Google Search queries hit an ‘all time high’ last quarter
author: Jay Peters
content:
![An image of Sundar Pichai in front of a Google logo](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/23951561/VRG_Illo_STK179_L_Normand_SundarPichai_Neutral.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google Search queries hit an "all time high" in the first quarter of 2026, according to a statement from CEO Sundar Pichai published [as part of Alphabet's earnings on Wednesday](https://s206.q4cdn.com/479360582/files/doc_financials/2026/q1/2026q1-alphabet-earnings-release.pdf).

"Our AI investments and full stack approach are lighting up every part of the business," Pichai says. "Search had a strong quarter with AI experiences driving usage, queries at an all time high, and 19% revenue growth." He also notes that Q1 was "our strongest quarter ever for our consumer AI plans, driven by the Gemini App" and that the company now has more than 350 million paid subscriptions, with "YouTube and Google One being the key drivers."

Alphabet also anno …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/920815/google-alphabet-q1-2026-earnings-sundar-pichai)

creator: Aarian Marshall
title: (No AI) Emergency First Responders Say Waymos Are Getting Worse
dc:creator: Aarian Marshall
content: “I believe the technology was deployed too quickly in too vast amounts, with hundreds of vehicles, when it wasn’t really ready,” one police official told federal regulators last month.
contentSnippet: “I believe the technology was deployed too quickly in too vast amounts, with hundreds of vehicles, when it wasn’t really ready,” one police official told federal regulators last month.
